name: Carol Burnett
abstract: Carol Creighton Burnett (born April 26, 1933) is an American comedian, actress, singer and writer. Burnett has played dramatic and comedic roles on stage and screen.

concerts.label: Julie and Carol at Carnegie Hall

works.t: The Most Happy Fella: Big D

concerts.label: The Toyota Comedy Festival: A Command Performance for Carol Burnett

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Hardaway
about_quotes.evidence: when we were doing the Carol Burnett Show... Carol Burnett would have wonderful Christmas parties... We had Basie’s band one time, this was for the cast and crew of the show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Hardaway interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 14, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Harold Ousley
about_quotes.evidence: Carol Burnett had a party out in California that the band was invited to. So everywhere that Basie went it was a top place. Same way with Lionel. So you get to experience that and meet people
about_quotes.doc_title: Harold Ousley interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: And then all the variety shows started, the Carol Burnett, the Glen Campbell the — oh hell — there were about a dozen variety shows, and they always used big bands.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Uh-huh. And you did the Lena Horne >> [(prose)] special and the Carol Burnett show for Mel Torme and Carol Burnett. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Foster
about_quotes.evidence: We did a number of television shows at the time. The Jackie Gleason Show, The Dinah Shore Show, The Garry Moore Show, Carol Burnett.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Foster — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: I did The Flip Wilson Show... and The Carol Burnett Show, I did some Fender bass on there.
